title: Cactus procerus

intro:
Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about the plant Cactus procerus. However, I found that the plant you are referring to might be "Peruvian Apple Cactus" (Cereus peruvianus), also known as Cactus procerus in some sources. 

Here's a paragraph about the Peruvian Apple Cactus:

The Peruvian Apple Cactus (Cactus procerus) is a species of cactus that belongs to the Cactaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with the Peruvian Apple Cactus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
